element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Foundation mechanic for all subsequent levels
Upgraded weapon for later level challenges
Environmental obstacle requiring precise platforming avoidance
Mobile enemy with larger hitbox; higher avoidance difficulty
Named boss enemy requiring specific strategies

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Building difficulty curve with early safe exploration, mid-game hazard introduction, and late-game boss encounters. Japanese version features reduced difficulty spike in Stage 2-2 with fewer spikes/pits.

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction of basic platforming and gizmo controls in safe environments (laboratory setting). Weapon acquisition establishes combat capacity.
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Environmental hazards (spikes, pits) introduced in Stage 2-2. Enemy variety expands with frag mine introduction and increased enemy density.
difficulty_curve.late_game: Boss encounters including Mohawk gremlin require mastery of weapons and avoidance mechanics. Goal progression toward Gremlin Control Center.
notable_spikes: Stage 2-2: Significant spike/pit introduction (US/EU version; JP version is notably reduced)
Stage 2-3: Mohawk boss encounter with aggressive attack patterns

notable_level_patterns.description: Levels advance through different sections of the Gremlins-infested building, with each area introducing new hazard types and enemy combinations.
New weapons (super tomato, matchstick bow) become available at specific junctures, enabling progression through subsequent hazard-dense areas.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Early laboratory sections with tomato; later sections with bow

notable_level_patterns.description: Stage 2-3 features Mohawk gremlin boss as definitive encounter, requiring synthesis of acquired combat and platforming skills.
Spikes and pits introduced together in Stage 2-2, with Japanese version using reduced frequency for difficulty balancing.

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor) - Gremlins 2: The New Batch (NES)
wikipedia - Gremlins 2: The New Batch (video game)
